steps.title: Identify the controlling claim
steps.content: The poem's strongest signal is “To take us Lands away”—books metaphorically transport readers to new places, rendered through journey and vehicle metaphors throughout.

steps.title: Recognize the complete idea
steps.content:
The poem specifies that this metaphorical transport is a universal resource: “This Traverse may the poorest take / Without oppress of Toll— / How frugal is the Chariot” establishes accessibility regardless of wealth.

C captures the full picture—literature transports people to new places and is available regardless of their economic means.
  • A introduces cultural connections never mentioned
  • B misframes accessibility as defiance rather than celebration
  • D contrives a comparison between reading and actual travel


That matches Choice C — Literature can metaphorically transport people to new places regardless of their means..
